methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $17M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Floodbase is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Bessie Schwarz and Dr. Beth Tellman, having raised $17M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine private companies and one public comparable (LNZA) operating in the same sector and stage, including Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy.

Two triangulation methods are applicable here. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $17M total raised as a proxy baseline, implying a valuation of $34M. Peer-median valuation examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $150M, ranging from $110M (Steady Energy) to $200M (Graphyte). This wide peer range reflects significant heterogeneity in Series A climate-tech valuations.

Triangulating these two methods yields a low of $29M, a median of $92M, and a high of $172M. The substantial spread between stage-progression ($34M) and peer-median ($150M) results reflects tension between cost-of-capital and market comparables. Key uncertainties driving this range include: definitional alignment of Floodbase's stage versus peer classification, geographic and customer-concentration differences, and whether peer valuations were fundraise-driven or independent assessments. A valuation in the $60M–$120M range may be defensible pending deeper diligence on traction metrics, customer acquisition cost, and revenue trajectory relative to the peer set.
